segments.caption: The video segment presents a continuous view of the DxO PhotoLab 4 Interface, showcasing a detailed photo editing session. segments.caption: The video segment begins with the DxO PhotoLab 4 Interface displaying a close-up image of green foliage with white flowers. The interface shows panels for histogram, metadata, and basic adjustment tools on the left and right sides, with the image centered in the workspace. The Metadata panel is expanded, revealing detailed EXIF information including camera model (NIKON Z 7), ISO 800, shutter speed 1/200 s, focal length 70 mm, and file path. The Basic Tools panel remains unchanged with all sliders at default values. The History panel at the bottom shows thumbnails of the current image and two other files. The Nik Collection logo is consistently visible in the bottom right corner, and the 'Export to Disk' button is present at the bottom right of the interface. The user then clicks on the Nik Collection logo, which triggers a Plugin Selector dialog box to appear over the image. This dialog lists various Nik Collection plugins such as Analog Efex Pro 2, Color Efex Pro 4, Dfine2, HDR Efex Pro 2, Sharpener Pro 3 Output Sharpening, Sharpener Pro 3 Raw Presharpening, Perspective Efex, Viveza 3, Silver Efex Pro 3, and Settings. The user selects 'Viveza 3' from the list, causing the dialog to close and the interface to transition to the Viveza 3 editing environment. In the Viveza 3 interface, a pop-up message appears titled 'Things to know before working with a TIFF file,' explaining that since the image was imported in TIFF format, the user can resume editing later by enabling an option via a checkbox next to the image information. The message includes an 'OK' button and a 'Do not show again' checkbox. The Viveza 3 interface displays the same foliage image split into a before-and-after comparison view, with the left side showing the original and the right side showing the edited version. The left panel lists presets like '000 Neutral,' '001 Golden Hour,' and '002 Blue Hour,' while the right panel shows adjustment options such as Loupe, Histogram, Global Adjustments, White Balance, Selective Adjustments, Control Points, and Levels & Curves. The bottom of the screen indicates the file being modified is '_DSC0088_Nik.tiff' from DxO PhotoLab, with camera details (24.0 MP, ISO 800, NIKON CORPORATION, NIKON Z 7) displayed. The 'Apply' and 'Cancel' buttons are visible at the bottom right, along with a 'Save preset' button. The pop-up message remains on screen throughout the remainder of the segment, with no further user interaction.
